---
title: "Comparing The Main Methods"
---

# Purpose

This note will compare the four main methods in the series on a common set of dimensions so readers can move from identification logic to design choice.

# Comparison Dimensions

## Identification Strategy

- Matching and weighting: selection on observables
- Difference-in-differences: change over time relative to comparison groups
- Synthetic control: weighted counterfactual trajectory
- Regression discontinuity: local comparison at a threshold

## Main Assumption

- Matching and weighting: no important unobserved confounding after conditioning
- Difference-in-differences: parallel trends
- Synthetic control: strong pre-treatment fit and no treated-unit-specific confounding shocks at treatment time
- Regression discontinuity: continuity at the cutoff and no manipulation

## Data Structure

- Cross-sectional or panel for matching and weighting
- Panel or repeated cross-section for DiD
- Long panel for synthetic control
- Cross-sectional or panel with a running variable for RD

## Diagnostic Emphasis

- Balance and overlap
- Pre-trends
- Pre-treatment fit and placebos
- Density and continuity checks

## Main Limitation

- Matching and weighting: hidden bias
- Difference-in-differences: trend violations
- Synthetic control: poor donor support
- Regression discontinuity: local external validity

# Planned Output

This page should eventually include a one-table summary plus short method-selection guidance linked to concrete evaluation scenarios.
